mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in FEDFUNDS from the current restrictive level of 5.33% signals the start of an easing cycle, lowering the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.33% just prior to first anticipated 2024 rate cut
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS month-over-month change and forward 20d SPX return stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or SPX returns are not significantly higher during FEDFUNDS decline periods (t-test p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises above 4.3% after having climbed from a cycle low (currently 4.2%), it signals labor-market deterioration consistent with early recession dynamics, causing SPX to underperform over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations are revised down.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 4.3% having increased at least 0.5pp from its trailing 12-month low
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.2% in latest snapshot, up from cycle lows near 3.5%
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances following UNRATE crossing 4.3%, or Rank IC < 0.03 across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous FEDFUNDS declines and rising UNRATE indicate the Fed is cutting rates reactively in response to labor deterioration rather than proactively for stabilization, which markets interpret as a recession warning, producing negative SPX forward returns over 20 trading days despite the rate cuts.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls month-over-month while UNRATE simultaneously rises month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:Fed poised to cut from 5.33% while UNRATE has risen to 4.2%
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns during joint FEDFUNDS-decline/UNRATE-rise periods are not significantly different from unconditional baseline returns (p>0.10), or effect disappears when controlling for FEDFUNDS alone.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in RSAFS reflects resilient consumer spending, which supports near-term corporate revenue expectations and produces positive SPX forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 709,668 million, near recent highs
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS month-over-month growth and forward 5d SPX return is below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO 3-month annualized growth turns negative, it signals an industrial contraction that precedes broader earnings weakness, generating negative SPX forward returns over the following 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month annualized growth rate falls below 0%
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 102.8887, near flat growth trajectory in recent months
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when INDPRO 3-month annualized growth is negative, or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening regime where FEDFUNDS remains above 5%, a decline in HOUST reflects rate-sensitive housing weakness that leads broader demand contraction, causing negative SPX forward returns over the next 60 trading days; this relationship is muted or reversed once FEDFUNDS falls below 4%.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines month-over-month while FEDFUNDS remains above 5%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,238k units with FEDFUNDS at 5.33%
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns following HOUST declines under high FEDFUNDS are not significantly negative (p>0.10), or the effect persists unchanged once FEDFUNDS drops below 4%, invalidating regime conditionality.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in PAYEMS growth and persistently elevated CPIAUCSL (sticky inflation) signal a stagflationary combination where the Fed cannot ease aggressively despite labor weakness, compressing equity valuations and producing negative SPX forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 158,779k showing slowing job gains while CPIAUCSL remains at 314.121
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative when PAYEMS 3-month growth decelerates and CPIAUCSL YoY inflation stays above 3%, or single-series PAYEMS or CPIAUCSL models alone explain the same variance (no incremental interaction benefit).

mechanism_narrative: During an early easing regime where FEDFUNDS begins declining while CPIAUCSL continues to decelerate (disinflation), the combination signals a soft-landing narrative that boosts risk appetite, producing positive SPX forward returns over the next 5 trading days; outside this regime the effect is absent.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 314.121 showing decelerating inflation with FEDFUNDS poised to decline from 5.33%
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns during simultaneous FEDFUNDS decline and CPIAUCSL deceleration are not significantly higher than unconditional baseline (p>0.10), or the pattern reverses in subsequent easing episodes.
